message.reasoning_content:
We are asked: "Accept an item only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence."

Candidate: cand_b214a8b69b498e6c, knowledge_type: operational_sequence, title: "AHU Mode Hierarchy", section_id: "5.15.1", summary: "When Zone Groups served by an air-handling system are in different modes, the highest mode in the hierarchy sets the AHU mode.", evidence_quote: "When Zone Group served by an air -handling system are in diffe rent modes, the following hierarchy applies (highest one sets AHU mode ): a. Occupied Mode b. Cooldown Mode c. Setup Mode d. Warmup Mode e. Setback Mode f. Unoccupied Mode", source_pages: [111].

This is clearly operational knowledge from ASHRAE Guideline 36, specifically section 5.15.1. It's useful, grounded, not front matter, TOC, generic definition, unsupported, or duplicate. So it should be accepted. The verdict is true. Provide short reason. Output only the JSON.

Thus: {"verdicts":[{"candidate_id":"cand_b214a8b69b498e6c","is_valid_guideline36_knowledge":true,"reason":"Valid operational sequence specifying AHU mode hierarchy from section 5.15.1.","category_if_not":null}]}
